skill1.kitLine: Activates when entering Full Burst. Affects all allies.
skill1.where: skill1[0] trigger:fullBurstEnter target:allies; skill1[1] trigger:fullBurstEnter target:allies
skill1.note: Two blocks share this trigger: block A (escalating buffs) and block B (heal).

skill1.kitLine: Effects vary according to the number of times used. Each subsequent effect triggers all effects before it:
skill1.note: Engine `escalating` natively models cumulative ramp. Only 2 steps encoded (tier 3 is last and skipped — see debuff cleanse below).

skill1.kitLine: Once: Potency of HP ▲ 30.96% for 5 sec.
skill1.where: unmodeled.skill1[0]; skill1[0].effects[0].steps[0] is a value-0 attackDamagePct placeholder (1s) for tier ordering only
skill1.note: Heal potency has no engine StatKey. Placeholder exists solely so Distributed Damage lands on 2nd FB, not 1st. No magnitude encoded.
Faithful value and duration. Fires on 2nd+ FB entry via escalating ramp.

skill1.kitLine: Three times: Stack count of debuffs ▼ 1.
skill1.note: Debuff cleanse — genuinely skippable: partless boss applies no modeled debuffs. As the LAST tier, omitting it doesn't shift tiers 1-2.

skill1.kitLine: Activates when entering Full Burst while an ally from the same squad is on the battlefield. Affects all allies.
skill1.note: Same-squad gate condition. Modeled as always-satisfied (no squad data in characters.json). The heal effect under this gate IS modeled (skill1[1]); only the gate semantics are unmodeled.

skill1.kitLine: Recovers 3.04% of the skill user's Max HP every 1 sec for 8 sec.
skill1.note: 8 timed recovery events per FB entry (engine gap #1 fix, 2026-07-17). Event-only — no HP magnitude (hard rule 2). Drives on-recovery consumers.

skill2.kitLine: Activates when Full Burst ends. Affects all allies.
Effects vary according to the number of times used. Each subsequent effect triggers all effects before it:
skill2.note: All 3 tiers encoded as escalating steps.
Faithful value/duration. hitRatePct is engine-inert (no core-rate conversion); the real core-hit-rate lift is unmeasured (⚑ flag 3).

skill2.kitLine: Twice: ATK ▲ 35.02% of the skill user's ATK for 10 sec.
skill2.note: casterAtkPct = flat add of HER Supporter ATK. Correctly 'of the skill user's ATK', not plain atkPct. Fires on 2nd+ FB-end.

skill2.kitLine: Three times: Reload Speed ▲ 40.04% for 15 sec.
skill2.note: Weapon-state = damage buff (hard rule 1). Faithful value/duration. Fires on 3rd+ FB-end.

burst.kitLine: Storage: Stores excess healing received by the skill user, up to 60.19% of their Max HP. Lasts for 25 sec.
burst.note: Self overheal buffer — defensive, no engine vocabulary, no damage consumer. Deliberately NOT encoded as shield (would falsely fire shielded triggers on Naga-class consumers). Re-open only if a storage-consumer kit appears.

burst.kitLine: Recovers 40.18% of the skill user's Max HP as HP.
burst.note: Event-only (hard rule 2) — no HP magnitude. Single recovery event on burst cast.

burst.kitLine: ATK ▲ 30.09% of the skill user's ATK for 10 sec.
burst.note: casterAtkPct = her Supporter ATK. Faithful value/duration.

documentedConfounds.text: skill1: 'same squad' heal gate modeled as always-satisfied — if it requires a lore-squad teammate (squad membership not in our data, unverified), recovery events over-fire in other teams (affects on-recovery consumers only)
documentedConfounds.why: If the gate should be OFF (no squadmate present), sim emits 8 recovery events/FB that shouldn't happen → on-recovery consumers (Crown-type) get inflated uptime → sim overestimates team damage. No impact on AIM's own damage or teams without recovery consumers.

documentedConfounds.text: skill1: the 8-tick regen (every 1s for 8s) now emits 8 timed recovery events (heal ticks:8) — on-recovery consumers (Crown-type) stay refreshed across the whole window (engine gap #1 fix, 2026-07-17)
documentedConfounds.why: Resolved. Block now has ticks:8 intervalSec:1, matching the kit text. The note's ⚑ flag 4 ('est. 1 recovery event/FB vs true 8') is stale — the fix is in the block and caveat.

documentedConfounds.text: skill1: tier-1 'Potency of HP' is a value-0 placeholder kept only for escalating tier order (heal potency has no engine stat)
documentedConfounds.why: Value-0 buff with 1s duration — produces no numerical effect. Exists solely to keep Distributed Damage on tier 2. No gap.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: Hit Rate ▲ 10.13% modeled as hitRatePct (engine-inert); its core-hit-rate lift is unmeasured
documentedConfounds.why: hitRatePct does nothing in the engine. In-game it provides some core-rate lift (proven mechanic, hard rule 4), so the sim is missing a small damage contribution → sim underestimates. Magnitude unknown (⚑ flag 3), likely small (k·10.13%, k≪1).

documentedConfounds.text: burst: healing Storage (60.19% Max HP overheal buffer) is unmodeled — defensive, no engine vocabulary; deliberately not encoded as a shield event
documentedConfounds.why: Purely defensive overheal buffer with no damage consumer in the current kit pool. No impact on damage output. Would only matter if a storage-consumer kit is added.

documentedConfounds.text: cadence: pullsPerSec / reloadFrames 141 / bolt-gap-vs-autofire are unverified datamine values (parser baseline)
documentedConfounds.why: Affects only AIM's own shot cadence and burst gauge generation. As a Supporter her personal damage is negligible vs her team buffs. Could swing her own shots ~15-20% (⚑ flag 2) but board impact is minimal.

documentedConfounds.text: PARSER BASELINE (HYPOTHESIS — NOT a validated model). Every ⚑ is an UNMEASURED estimate; hand-tune + record against a real fight before trusting any number.
documentedConfounds.why: Global banner: the entire override is unvalidated. No board data exists to confirm or deny accuracy. All values are parser-derived, not measured.

authorIntentSummary: The override models Anchor: Innocent Maid as a healer-supporter whose value is team buffs and heal events, not personal damage. S1 uses an escalating block (2 steps: value-0 placeholder + distributedDamagePct) on fullBurstEnter plus an 8-tick heal event; S2 uses a 3-step escalating block on fullBurstEnd (hitRatePct/casterAtkPct/reloadSpeedPct); burst emits a single heal event + casterAtkPct. Key approximations: the same-squad gate is always-satisfied (unverifiable), hitRatePct is engine-inert, tier-1 heal potency and burst Storage are deliberately unmodeled (no engine vocabulary, no damage consumer). The entire file is a parser-baseline hypothesis with zero board validation — no board data exists for this unit.
